summary_text: **Findings** 1. Medium: There is a policy tension with “no extra automation.” These lines broadly allow backup dispatch: [~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:21](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:21), [~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:108](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:108), [~/.codex/prompts/vote.md:10](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md:10), [~/.codex/prompts/vote.md:81](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md:81). This can be read as permitting auto-dispatch outside the completion-specific guard. 2. Info: The intended completion behavior is explicitly present in all four command contracts: [~/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:75](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/kernel.md:75), [~/.codex/prompts/k.md:75](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/k.md:75), [~/.codex/prompts/vote.md:62](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/vote.md:62), [~/.codex/prompts/v.md:62](/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts/v.md:62). 3. Info: Cross-memory is aligned with the same policy: [~/.claude/memory/cross-orchestrator-memory.md:40](/Users/masayuki/.claude/memory/cross-orchestrator-memory.md:40), [~/.claude/memory/cross-orchestrator-memory.md:41](/Users/masayuki/.claude/memory/cross-orchestrator-memory.md:41). 4. Info: Parity holds exactly: `kernel.md == k.md`, `vote.md == v.md` (no diff). **Need further edit?** No mandatory edit is needed to satisfy the stated completion-time policy. If you want stricter “no extra automation” semantics globally, one wording tighten around the “Backup-only dispatch is allowed” lines would reduce ambiguity. **Residual risks** - Ambiguous precedence between “dispatch is allowed” vs “do not auto-dispatch unless contract requires.” - No regression check enforcing alias parity and key-policy-string parity across `kernel/k` and `vote/v` (future drift risk).
